svg.text: Context · Memory · Prompt engineering for team AI-partnered software dev
Explicit memory + prompt layers · parallel work via strands
Portable scaffold — works in Claude Code and Cline
Phase prompts tuned with EIP (Emotional Intelligence Prompting)
Markdown files as the shared team memory
State lives in the repo, not the chat
everything COMPACT needs lives in markdown files the team can read, diff, and review
Identity: who / why / scope / Contributors table
Active phase · Done / In progress / Next · Flags
Module table + dep graph — regenerated, never hand-edited
Parallel in-flight work — STRAND.md + journal + drafts
Public surface · functions/types callers rely on
Key choices · links to [D-XXX] in DECISIONS.md
Non-goals · what this module deliberately does NOT do
Depends on / Depended on by · edges of the dep graph
HARD — signature / invariant / non-goal / dep change
The actual code — must honor the MODULE.md contract
above it, and the FR/NFR entries it claims to serve.
Each layer is authored and audited separately.
the user picks which layer changes to resolve each drift.
Deferred items (in any layer) are NOT drift — surfaced separately.
each phase loads an EIP-tuned persona + different artifacts — switch with /switch-phase <name>
Probe the problem, surface assumptions, avoid solutioning.
v1 scope + users + success criteria present;
FR set stable enough to start architecting against.
Do NOT pre-load MODULE.md files — scope creep.
Senior architect + design partner. Design doc-first,
one module at a time. Show trade-offs, flag over-engineering.
requirements.md on demand · peer MODULE.md on demand
Every planned module has a MODULE.md draft;
every FR/NFR has ≥1 owning module; dep graph acyclic.
No separate "risk register" — risks live in DECISIONS or STATUS Flags.
Senior engineering partner. Build in small pieces,
honor the MODULE.md contract, flag contract drift.
requirements.md — Tier-2, only when task cites an FR/NFR
Feature works · tests pass · MODULE.md contracts honored
· no unresolved hard-flag changes in working tree.
About to change a curated section? STOP. Switch to architecture first.
the commands you invoke — each writes a narrow, documented surface
Scaffold a new project (or --retrofit an existing one).
STATUS.md — Active phase + Last updated · STRAND.md if bound
MAP.md · MODULE.md Structure sections (regen markers only)
audit MODULE.md deltas, regen map, run doctor, commit.
strand journal + drafts (if bound) · else STATUS.md + DECISIONS.md
Interactive — user picks direction per drift.
only on user-approved resolutions — layer the user picked
references, retrofit scope. Read-only — reports, no edits.
nothing — prints findings for maintainer to fix
/start-strand <name> scaffold a strand · /switch-strand <name> bind session · /list-strands active + archived · /land-strand <name> archive + promote drafts
A strand is one folder of in-flight state (STRAND.md + journal.md + decisions-draft.md). Land = archive to strands/_archive/, architect promotes drafts to DECISIONS.md.
how the skills compose for the common scenarios
Start from scratch. Interview drives scaffold generation.
RETROFIT — early-stage codebase (has reqs + some design, little code)
Reconcile design intent with observed code. Requirement IDs preserved verbatim.
RETROFIT — ongoing codebase (has reqs + design + significant code)
Prioritize architecture curation; `<!-- retrofit: skeleton -->` sentinel gives grace period from drift-check.
Day-to-day loop. Memory only gets made at /close-session — everything mid-session is working tree.
Invoke when R/D/I layers may have diverged, or before a release. Interactive — user drives every resolution.
PARALLEL WORK — multiple strands in flight
Each strand is its own folder of state. Sessions bind to a strand; close-session writes there. Architect lands drafts sequentially.
5 · WHAT'S IN CONTEXT AT ANY TIME
progressive loading — each phase is narrow on purpose; more context ≠ better reasoning
DECISIONS.md (read on triage, not on entry)
all other MODULE.md files · code bodies
DECISIONS.md (reach in only on cited choice)
CORE PRINCIPLE · Files are the truth. Chat is scratch space. Memory gets made at /close-session — never mid-session.
